title: Red Hat Security Advisory: fontforge security update
overview:
An update for fontforge is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CodeReady Linux Builder (v. 9)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: fontforge security update

An update for fontforge is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for fontforge is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.src.rpm
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.i686.rpm
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
fontforge-debuginfo-20201107-7.el9_7.i686.rpm
fontforge-debuginfo-20201107-7.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
fontforge-debugsource-20201107-7.el9_7.i686.rpm
fontforge-debugsource-20201107-7.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
fontforge-debuginfo-20201107-7.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
fontforge-debugsource-20201107-7.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.aarch64.rpm
fontforge-debuginfo-20201107-7.el9_7.aarch64.rpm
fontforge-debugsource-20201107-7.el9_7.aarch64.rpm
fontforge-20201107-7.el9_7.s390x.rpm
fontforge-debuginfo-20201107-7.el9_7.s390x.rpm
fontforge-debugsource-20201107-7.el9_7.s390x.rpm

CVE-2025-15269: A flaw was found in FontForge. This use-after-free vulnerability, occurring during the parsing of SFD (Spline Font Database) files, allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code. Successful exploitation requires user interaction, such as opening a specially crafted malicious file or visiting a malicious web page. This can lead to complete control over the affected system.

CVE-2025-15275: A flaw was found in FontForge. A remote attacker could exploit a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability during SFD file parsing. This issue arises from insufficient validation of user-supplied data length before copying it to a buffer. Successful exploitation requires user interaction, such as opening a malicious SFD file, and could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user.

CVE-2025-15279: A flaw was found in FontForge. This heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability occurs during the parsing of pixels within BMP (Bitmap) files, due to insufficient validation of user-supplied data length. A remote attacker could exploit this by tricking a user into opening a malicious BMP file or visiting a malicious page. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user.
